articles.title: LAUSD Votes to Slash Screen Time, Joining a Growing State-Level Movement
articles.summary: A parent's frustration over an iPad penalty in PE class sparked a campaign that just convinced the nation's second-largest school district to restrict screens. Alabama, Tennessee, Utah, and Virginia have already passed laws, with more states considering similar limits.

articles.title: Global May Day Rallies Target War-Driven Cost-of-Living Crisis
articles.summary: From Seoul to São Paulo, workers took to the streets on International Workers' Day to protest rising living costs tied to the Iran war. In Manila, demonstrators clashed with police near the U.S. Embassy, while European unions declared, 'Working people refuse to pay the price for Donald Trump's war.'

articles.title: EU-Mercosur Trade Pact Takes Effect, Creating a $22 Trillion Market
articles.summary: After a quarter-century of negotiations, the EU and South America's Mercosur bloc provisionally launched one of the world's largest free trade zones, covering 720 million consumers. The deal eliminates tariffs on over 90% of bilateral trade but faces legal challenges and fierce opposition from farmers and environmentalists.
